title: agrostis alba var. prorepens
intro: Agrostis alba var. prorepens, also known as creeping meadow-grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions and wounds, due to its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Agrostis alba var. prorepens include reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and promoting tissue repair. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into a tincture, infusion, or salve, which can be applied topically to benefit from its medicinal properties.
